blocks:
Surat al-Hadid (The Iron) is Medinan

Its number of verses is twenty-nine according to the Kufan count and twenty-eight according to the others Its points of difference Two verses: مِن قِبَلِهِ الْعَذابُ and الْإِنْجِيلَ according to the Basran count بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (1) Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah. It is said that it is mentioned here, and in al-Hashr and al-Saff, using the past tense form, while in al-Jumu'ah and al-Taghabun, it is mentioned using the present tense form. This is an indication that it is the nature of that which is attributed to Him to glorify Him at all times, because it is an inherent, natural indication (دلالة جبليّة) that does not change with the change of circumstances. And the occurrence of the absolute infinitive (المصدر مطلقاً) in Bani Isra'il (Surah 17) is more eloquent in that it indicates, through its absoluteness, the deservingness of glorification (استحقاق التسبيح) from everything and in every state. The fact that it is made transitive using the lām (لِلَّهِ), even though it is inherently transitive by itself, is an indication that the performance of the action is for the sake of Allah and purely for His Countenance. وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ** (1) And He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. This is an indication of that which is the origin (المبدأ) of the glorification.

(2) لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (To Him belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth.) For He is the Creator of them and the Disposer of affairs within them. ** يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ** (He gives life and brings death, and He has power over all things.) (3) هُوَ الْأَوَّلُ ** (He is the First) before everything, وَالْآخِرُ ** (and the Last) after everything, and the Manifest (الظَّاهِرُ), dominant over everything through His subjugation (القهر) of it, and the Immanent (الْباطِنُ), the Knower (الخبير) of the inner reality (بباطن) of everything. And He is also the First (الأوّل) and the Last (الآخر); from Him the causes (الأسباب) begin, and to Him the effects (المسبّبات) conclude. And the Manifest (الظاهر) and the Immanent (الباطن): the Manifest is He Whose existence is evident from everything, and the Immanent is the reality of His Essence (حقيقة ذاته), which the intellects (العقول) cannot fathom. In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said in a sermon of His: "He Whose beginning (أوليّته) has no end (نهاية), and Whose finality (آخريَّته) has neither limit (حدّ) nor goal (غاية)." And He said: "He Who is Immanent (بطن) from the hidden matters (خفيّات الأمور) and Manifest (ظهر) to the intellects (العقول) through what is seen in His creation of the signs of governance (علامات التدبير)." ** وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ ** (and He has knowledge of all things.) The manifest (الظاهر) and the hidden (الخفي) are equal in His sight.

(٤) هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ** (4) He is the One who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then settled on the Throne. Its exegesis has already passed in Surat al-A'raf. يَعْلَمُ ما يَلِجُ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (He knows what penetrates the earth) such as seeds (كالبذور), وَما يَخْرُجُ مِنْها ** (and what emerges from it,) such as crops (كالزروع), وَما يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (and what descends from the sky,) such as rains (كالامطار), وَما يَعْرُجُ فِيها ** (and what ascends to it.) such as vapors (كالابخرة). وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ ** (He is with you wherever you may be,) His knowledge and power are never separated from you under any circumstance. وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (and Allah is watchful of what you do.) so He will recompense you for it.

(٥) لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (5) His is the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. He mentioned this alongside the return (recreation) just as He mentioned it alongside the initiation (creation), because it is like a prelude to both of them. وَإِلَى اللهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ. and to Allah all matters are returned. (٦) يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهارِ وَيُولِجُ النَّهارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَهُوَ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ ** (6) He makes the night pass into the day, and makes the day pass into the night, and He is aware of what is in the breasts. —with their hidden contents (مكنوناتها). (٧) آمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَأَنْفِقُوا مِمَّا جَعَلَكُمْ مُسْتَخْلَفِينَ فِيهِ ** (7) Believe in Allah and His Apostle, and spend out of what He has made you successors to. [This refers to] the wealth (الأموال) over which Allah has made you successors (خلفاء) in its disposal, for in reality, it belongs to Him, not to you; or [it refers to the wealth] over which He made you successors (استخلفكم) after those who came before you, in possessing it and disposing of it. And in this (statement) is a depreciation (توهين) of spending upon the self. فَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ وَأَنْفَقُوا لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ ** So those of you who have faith and spend, there will be a great reward for them. A promise in which there are exaggerations (i.e., great emphasis).

(٨) وَما لَكُمْ لا تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالرَّسُولُ يَدْعُوكُمْ لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ ** (8) What is the matter with you that you do not believe in Allah, while the Apostle invites you to believe in your Lord? meaning: What excuse do you have for abandoning faith (الإيمان) while the Messenger invites you to it with proofs (الحجج) and signs (الآيات)? وَقَدْ أَخَذَ مِيثاقَكُمْ ** (while He has already taken your covenant) And Allah had already taken your covenant (ميثاقكم) regarding faith (الإيمان) before that. And it was read based on the passive construction (على البناء للمفعول). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** (if you are believers.) for some compelling reason (موجب), for this is a compelling reason (موجب) beyond which there is no increase.

(٩) هُوَ الَّذِي يُنَزِّلُ عَلى عَبْدِهِ آياتٍ بَيِّناتٍ لِيُخْرِجَكُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ ** (9) It is He who sends down manifest signs to His servant that He may bring you out from darkness into light. from the darknesses (ظلمات) of disbelief (الكفر) to the light (نور) of faith (الايمان). وَإِنَّ اللهَ بِكُمْ لَرَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ. (9) Indeed Allah is most kind and merciful to you. (١٠) وَما لَكُمْ أَلَّا تُنْفِقُوا ** (10) What is the matter with you that you do not spend And what thing is there for you in that you do not spend فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** (10) in the way of Allah, in that which is a means of proximity (قربة) to Him. وَلِلَّهِ مِيراثُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (10) while to Allah belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth? He inherits everything in them, and no wealth remains for anyone. And if that is the case, then spending it in a way that secures a lasting recompense (عوضاً يبقى), which is the reward (الثواب), is more appropriate (أولى). لا يَسْتَوِي مِنْكُمْ مَنْ أَنْفَقَ مِنْ قَبْلِ الْفَتْحِ وَقاتَلَ ** (10) Not equal among you are those who spent and fought before the victory. This is a clarification of the difference in rank (تفاوت) between those who spend and those who fight, due to the variation in their circumstances, such as precedence (السبق), strength of certainty (قوّة اليقين), and seeking out need (تحرّي الحاجة). Its counterpart (قسيمه) is omitted due to its clarity and the indication of what follows it. And the victory (الفتح) is the conquest of Mecca (فتح مكّة), since Islam gained strength (عزّ) through it, its adherents became numerous, and the need for fighting and spending decreased. أُولئِكَ أَعْظَمُ دَرَجَةً مِنَ الَّذِينَ أَنْفَقُوا مِنْ بَعْدُ ** (10) They are greater in rank than those who spent afterwards from after the victory (الفتح). وَقاتَلُوا وَكُلًّا ** (10) and fought. Yet to each And it was recited with the raf' (الرفع) case (nominative). وَعَدَ اللهُ الْحُسْنى ** (10) Allah has promised the best reward, the excellent reward (المثوبة الحسنى). وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ ** (10) and Allah is well aware of what you do. of its apparent (ظاهره) and its hidden (باطنه) aspects, and He will recompense you accordingly.

(١١) مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللهَ قَرْضاً حَسَناً ** (11) Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan? [This refers to] spending his wealth in His way, hoping that He will compensate him. And its goodness (حسنه) is achieved through sincerity (الإخلاص), seeking out the lawful (تحرّي الحلال), and [spending in] the best directions (أفضل الجهات) for Him, [despite] the love of wealth (محبّة المال) and the hope for life (رجاء الحياة). فَيُضاعِفَهُ لَهُ ** (that He may multiply it for him) So He gives him his reward manifold (أضعافاً). وَلَهُ أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ ** (and there is a noble reward for him) And that reward (الأجر) is noble in itself, even if it is not multiplied. And it was recited (وقرئ) as فَيُضاعِفَهُ with the nasb (بالنّصب) and يُضَعِّفَهُ (يضعّفه) both in the raf' (مرفوعاً) and the nasb (منصوباً).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the gift (صلة) to the Imam.

And in a narration in al-Kafi: [It was revealed] concerning the gift (صلة) to the Imam during the rule (دولة) of the transgressors (الفسّاق).

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah did not ask His creation for a loan from what is in their hands out of any need He has for that. Rather, whatever right belongs to Allah, it belongs only to His representative (لوليّه).

(١٢) يَوْمَ تَرَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ يَسْعى نُورُهُمْ ** (12) The day you see the faithful, men and women, their light proceeding [This light is] that by which they are guided to Paradise بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمانِهِمْ before them and on their right hand, from the direction where they are given the scrolls of their deeds. بُشْراكُمُ الْيَوْمَ جَنَّاتٌ ** 'Good news for you today: gardens'—that is said to them—تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها ذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ. with streams running in them, to remain in them forever.' That is the great success.

(١٣) يَوْمَ يَقُولُ الْمُنافِقُونَ وَالْمُنافِقاتُ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا انْظُرُونا ** (13) The day the hypocrites, men and women, will say to those who have faith, 'Wait for us! Wait for us (انتظرونا) or look at us (انظروا إلينا). And it was recited انْظِرُنا (look at us/wait for us), meaning: Grant us respite (أمهلونا), ** نَقْتَبِسْ مِنْ نُورِكُمْ قِيلَ ارْجِعُوا وَراءَكُمْ ** so that we may glean from your light.' They will be told, 'Go back behind you, to the world (إلى الدنيا), and seek (فَالْتَمِسُوا) light (نُوراً) by acquiring the divine cognitions (المعارف الإلهيّة), virtuous morals (الأخلاق الفاضلة), and righteous deeds (الأعمال الصالحة), for the light is generated from them. ** فَضُرِبَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِسُورٍ لَهُ بابٌ باطِنُهُ فِيهِ الرَّحْمَةُ ** and seek light.' Then a wall will be set up between them, having a gate, whose inner side contains mercy, with a wall (بحائط). because it adjoins Paradise (الجنة). ** وَظاهِرُهُ مِنْ قِبَلِهِ ** and whose outer side, facing them, contains punishment. from its direction (من جهته) ** الْعَذابُ ** because it adjoins the Fire (النّار).

(١٤) يُنادُونَهُمْ أَلَمْ نَكُنْ مَعَكُمْ ** (14) They will call out to them, 'Were we not with you?' They mean their agreement with them outwardly (في الظاهر). قالُوا بَلى وَلكِنَّكُمْ فَتَنْتُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ ** They will say, 'Yes, but you tempted yourselves, with hypocrisy (بالنفاق). And al-Qummi said: with acts of disobedience (بالمعاصي). وَتَرَبَّصْتُمْ بالمؤمنين الدوائر and you waited for misfortunes (الدوائر) to befall the believers (بالمؤمنين). وَارْتَبْتُمْ ** and you were skeptical, and you doubted (وشككتم) the religion (في الدين). وَغَرَّتْكُمُ الْأَمانِيُّ حَتَّى جاءَ أَمْرُ اللهِ ** and the [false] hopes deceived you until the command of Allah came, which is death (الموت). وَغَرَّكُمْ بِاللهِ الْغَرُورُ and the Deceiver deceived you concerning Allah.' The Deceiver (الْغَرُورُ) is Satan (الشيطان) or the world (الدنيا).

(١٥) ** فَالْيَوْمَ لا يُؤْخَذُ مِنْكُمْ فِدْيَةٌ ** (15) So today no ransom shall be taken from you a ransom (فداء) ** وَلا مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** nor from those who disbelieved. outwardly and inwardly ** مَأْواكُمُ ** Your refuge ** النَّارُ هِيَ مَوْلاكُمْ ** is the Fire. It is your master, al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It is more deserving of you. ** وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ ** and how evil is the destination! the Fire (النّار)

al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: The light (النور) will be divided among the people on the Day of Resurrection according to the measure of their faith. It will be divided for the hypocrite, and his light will be between the big toe of his left foot. He will look at his light, then he will say to the believers: "Stay in your place until I acquire some of your light." So the believers will say to them: ارْجِعُوا وَراءَكُمْ فَالْتَمِسُوا نُوراً (Go back behind you, and seek a light). Then they will return, and a wall (سور) will be struck between them. He said: By Allah, He did not mean by that the Jews nor the Christians, but He only meant by it the People of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة).

** أَلَمْ يَأْنِ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنْ تَخْشَعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ لِذِكْرِ اللهِ ** (16) Has the time not yet come for those who have faith that their hearts should be humbled for the remembrance of Allah

Has its time not come? وَما نَزَلَ مِنَ الْحَقِ (and what has been sent down of the truth), meaning: the Quran. وَلا يَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلُ (and that they should not be like those who were given the Book before). And it was recited with the yā’ (الياء). فَطالَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَمَدُ ** (and the term was prolonged for them), meaning: the time (الزمان). فَقَسَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَكَثِيرٌ مِنْهُمْ فاسِقُونَ ** (and so their hearts hardened, and many of them are transgressors), meaning: those who deviate (خارجون) from their religion.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: This verse was revealed concerning al-Qa'im (peace be upon him), the verse وَلا يَكُونُوا... [to the end of the verse].

I say: Perhaps the intended meaning is that it was revealed concerning the matter of the occultation (غيبة) of al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) and its believing people.

(١٧) اعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ يُحْيِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها. (17) Know that Allah revives the earth after its death. In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: Allah the Almighty revives it through al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) after its death, meaning by its death the disbelief (كفر) of its people, and the disbeliever (الكافر) is dead. And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Justice after oppression (الجور). And it was said: It is a metaphor for reviving the hardened hearts (القلوب القاسية) through remembrance (الذكر) and recitation (التلاوة). **قَدْ بَيَّنَّا لَكُمُ الْآياتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ ** (We have certainly made the signs clear for you, so that you may apply reason.) So that your intellects may be perfected.

(١٨) إِنَّ الْمُصَّدِّقِينَ وَالْمُصَّدِّقاتِ ** (18) Indeed, the charitable men and the charitable women, meaning the men who give charity (المتصدّقين) and the women who give charity (والمتصدّقات). And it was read with the lightening (تخفيف) of the ṣād, meaning those who affirmed/believed in (صدقوا) Allah and His Messenger. وَأَقْرَضُوا اللهَ قَرْضاً حَسَناً يُضاعَفُ لَهُمْ وَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ.** and those who lend Allah a good loan—it shall be multiplied for them, and there is a noble reward for them.

(١٩) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الصِّدِّيقُونَ وَالشُّهَداءُ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ. (And those who have faith in Allah and His apostles—it is they who are the truthful and the witnesses near their Lord.) In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "Verily, this is for us and for our followers (شيعتنا)."

In al-Mahasin, narrated from his father (peace be upon them both), he said: "There is none among our followers (شيعتنا) except that he is a truthful one (صدّيق) and a martyr (شهيد)." It was asked: "How can that be, when the majority of them die upon their beds?" He replied: "Do you not recite the Book of Allah in [Surah] al-Hadid: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الصِّدِّيقُونَ وَالشُّهَداءُ (And those who have faith in Allah and His apostles—it is they who are the truthful and the witnesses)? He said: "If the martyrs (الشهداء) were only as they say, the martyrs would be few."

In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "The deceased among our followers (شيعتنا) is a truthful one (صدّيق), who affirmed our command (أمرنا), and loved for our sake and hated for our sake, intending thereby Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. He believes in Allah and His Messenger." Then He recited this Ayah.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), who said: "The one among you who knows this matter (الامر), awaits it, and seeks reward (المحتسب) therein, is like one who strove in the way of Allah with the Qa'im (peace be upon him) with his sword." Then He said: "Nay, by Allah, [he is] like one who strove with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with his sword." Then He said a third time: "Nay, by Allah, [he is] like one who was martyred with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in His tent (فسطاطه). And there is an Ayah in the Book of Allah concerning you." It was asked: "And which Ayah?" He said: "The saying of Allah: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ (And those who have faith in Allah and His apostles...)—the Ayah." Then He said: "By Allah, you have become truthful ones (صادقين), witnesses (شهداء) near your Lord."

In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "Verily, the one among you who dies upon this matter (الامر) is a martyr (شهيد)." It was asked: "Even if he dies upon his bed?" He said: "Yes, by Allah, even if he dies upon his bed, he is alive near his Lord, receiving sustenance (يُرزَق)." And narrated from al-Hakam ibn Utaybah, who said: When the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) killed the Khawarij on the Day of Nahrawan, a man stood up to Him and said: "O Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), blessed are we, for we witnessed this stand with You and killed these Khawarij with You." The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "By Him Who split the seed and created the soul (النّسمة), people have witnessed this stand with us whose fathers and grandfathers Allah has not yet created." The man said: "And how have people witnessed us who have not been created?" He said: "Rather, they are a people who will be in the Last Time (آخر الزمان). They will share with us in what we are in, and they will submit to us. Thus, those are truly, truly our partners in it." And in a narration, He said: "Verily, people are gathered together by contentment (الرضا) and displeasure (السخط). So, whoever is content with a matter has entered into it, and whoever is displeased with it has exited from it."

** لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ وَنُورُهُمْ ** (They shall have their reward and their light) —the reward of the truthful ones (الصديقين) and the martyrs (الشهداء) and their light— وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَحِيمِ. (But those who are faithless and deny Our signs—they shall be the inmates of the Hellfire.)

**(٢٠) اعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ وَزِينَةٌ وَتَفاخُرٌ بَيْنَكُمْ وَتَكاثُرٌ فِي الْأَمْوالِ وَالْأَوْلادِ ** (20) Know that the life of this world is only play and diversion, and glitter, and boasting among you, and rivalry in respect of wealth and children.

When He mentioned the state of the two groups, He belittled the affairs of the world—meaning that by which one does not attain the bliss of the Hereafter—by clarifying that they are illusory affairs, devoid of benefit, and swift in vanishing. And they are only play (لَعِبٌ), in which people greatly tire themselves, like the tiring of children in playgrounds, without benefit; and diversion (لَهْوٌ) by which they distract themselves from what concerns them; and adornment (زِينَةٌ) consisting of desirable clothing, splendid mounts, lofty dwellings, and the like; and boasting (تَفاخُرٌ) through lineages (الأنساب) and merits (الاحساب); and rivalry in multitude (تَكاثُرٌ) through numbers (العدد) and equipment/provisions (العُدد). And these are six matters encompassing the desires of the world, which are not related to the Hereafter, arranged in mention according to the order of their usual occurrence for man.

**كَمَثَلِ غَيْثٍ أَعْجَبَ الْكُفَّارَ نَباتُهُ ثُمَّ يَهِيجُ فَتَراهُ مُصْفَرًّا ثُمَّ يَكُونُ حُطاماً ** like the example of rain whose vegetation impresses the tillers; then it withers, and you see it turn yellow, then it becomes chaff.

Then He established the belittlement of the world and likened it, regarding the swiftness of its passing and the scarcity of its benefit, to the state of vegetation which the rain caused to grow and which matured. So the guards (الحرّاس) or the disbelievers (الكافرون) in Allah were impressed by it, because they are more intensely impressed by the adornment of the world. This is because when the believer sees something impressive, his thought moves to the power of its Maker, and he is impressed by that [power], whereas the disbeliever's thought does not go beyond what he perceives, so he is immersed in admiration for it. Then it withered (هاج), meaning it dried up due to a blight (عاهة), and turned yellow (اصفرّ). Then it became chaff (حُطاماً), meaning brittle fragments (هشيما).

**وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَرِضْوانٌ ** And in the Hereafter there is a severe punishment, and forgiveness from Allah and His pleasure.

Then He magnified the affairs of the Hereafter and confirmed that, as a means of deterring from immersion in the world, and urging toward that which necessitates the honor of the ultimate outcome (العقبى).

**وَمَا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا إِلَّا مَتاعُ الْغُرُورِ ** The life of this world is nothing but the wares of delusion.

Meaning, for the one who turns toward it and does not seek the Hereafter through it.

(٢١) ** سابِقُوا ** سارعوا (21) Compete with one another Hasten (Race), with the racing of those who compete in the track, إِلى مَغْفِرَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** towards the things that necessitate it, وَجَنَّةٍ عَرْضُها كَعَرْضِ السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** like the breadth of their totality if they were spread out. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) (عليه السلام): That the lowest in rank among the people of Paradise is one whom, if the two weighty ones (الثقلان), the jinn (الجنّ) and mankind (الإنس), were to alight upon him, he would accommodate them with food and drink—the hadith. And it has preceded in Surat al-Hajj. أُعِدَّتْ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ ذلِكَ فَضْلُ اللهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ. (It has been prepared for those who have faith in Allah and His apostles. That is Allah’s grace, which He grants to whomever He wishes, and Allah is the possessor of the great grace.)

(٢٢) ما أَصابَ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (No affliction visits [anyone] in the earth) like drought and blight (وعاهة), وَلا فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ ** (nor in yourselves) like illness and affliction (وآفة), إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ ** (but it is in a Book) except that it is written, مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ نَبْرَأَها ** (before We bring it into being) We create it.

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Allah spoke the truth, and His messengers conveyed [the message]. His Book in the heaven is His knowledge of it, and His Book on earth is our knowledge (علومنا) during the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر) and at other times."

And in al-Ilal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Indeed, the angel of the wombs (ملك الأرحام) writes everything that afflicts man in this world between his eyes. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ما أَصابَ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ ** (No affliction visits [anyone] in the earth) [the rest of] the Ayah." ** إِنَّ ذلِكَ ** (That) that He establishes it in a Book, عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيرٌ ** (is easy for Allah) due to His independence therein from preparation (العدّة) and time (المدّة).

** لِكَيْلا تَأْسَوْا ** (So that you may not grieve) The Almighty's saying: ** لِكَيْلا تَأْسَوْا ** (So that you may not grieve) meaning: it was established and written so that you do not grieve عَلى ما فاتَكُمْ ** (over what has escaped you) of the blessings (نعم) of the world وَلا تَفْرَحُوا بِما آتاكُمْ ** (nor exult over what He has given you) meaning: what Allah has given you of it. For whoever knows that everything is predetermined (مقدّر), the matter becomes easy for him.

And it was read (وقرئ): فما أتاكم (and what has come to you) derived from الإتيان (coming/bringing), so that it corresponds to ما فاتَكُمْ (what has escaped you).

In Nahj al-Balaghah: الزهد (Asceticism/Detachment) is entirely contained between two phrases from the Quran. Allah the Almighty said: لِكَيْلا تَأْسَوْا عَلى ما فاتَكُمْ وَلا تَفْرَحُوا بِما آتاكُمْ ** (So that you may not grieve over what has escaped you, nor exult over what He has given you). And whoever does not grieve over the past and does not exult over what is coming (الآتي) has grasped الزهد (detachment) by both its ends.

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "Behold, الزهد (detachment) is in one verse of the Book of Allah." Then He recited this verse.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "It was revealed concerning Abu Bakr and his companions, one part preceding and one part following: لِكَيْلا تَأْسَوْا عَلى ما فاتَكُمْ (So that you may not grieve over what has escaped you) of that which was specifically designated for Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), وَلا تَفْرَحُوا بِما آتاكُمْ (nor exult over what He has given you) of the tribulation (الفتنة) that presented itself to you after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم."

وَاللهُ لا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتالٍ فَخُورٍ ** (and Allah does not like any swaggering braggart) And the Almighty's saying: وَاللهُ لا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتالٍ فَخُورٍ ** (and Allah does not like any swaggering braggart). In this, there is an indication (اشعار) that the grief (الاسى) intended is the grief that prevents submission (التسليم) to the command of Allah, and the joy (الفرح) intended is the joy that necessitates insolence (البطر) and cunning (الاحتيال), since few are those who keep themselves steadfast during hardship (الضرّاء) and ease (السرّاء).

(٢٤) الَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ ** (Those who are stingy and bid people to be stingy) It is an appositive (بدل) for "every proud one" (كلّ مختال), for indeed, the one who is proud of his wealth is usually stingy with it. Or it is a subject (مبتدأ) whose predicate is omitted, due to what follows indicating it. وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّ فَإِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ ** (And whoever turns away—Allah is indeed the All-sufficient, the All-laudable.) And whoever turns away from spending, then Allah is independent of him and of his spending, praiseworthy (محمود) in His essence (في ذاته). The turning away from thanking Him does not harm Him, nor does He benefit from drawing near to Him through any of His blessings. And in this is a threat (تهديد) and an indication that the command to spend is for the benefit of the spender. And it was recited (قرئ) as فانّ الله الغنيّ.

(٢٥) لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا رُسُلَنا بِالْبَيِّناتِ ** (25) We certainly sent Our apostles with manifest proofs [meaning] with arguments (بالحجج) and miracles (والمعجزات). وَأَنْزَلْنا مَعَهُمُ الْكِتابَ.** and We sent down with them the Book.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding this Ayah, the Book (الكتاب) is the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر) by which the knowledge of everything is known, which was with the Prophets. He said: And what is known of what is called the Book (الكتاب)—the Torah (التوراة), the Gospel (الإنجيل), and the Criterion (الفرقان)—contains the Book of Noah, and it contains the Book of Salih, Shu'ayb, and Abraham. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, informed [us]: إِنَّ هذا لَفِي الصُّحُفِ الْأُولى صُحُفِ إِبْراهِيمَ وَمُوسى (This is indeed in the former scriptures, the scriptures of Abraham and Moses) (Q. 87:18-19). So where are the scriptures (صحف) of Abraham (peace be upon him)? The scriptures of Abraham are only the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر), and the scriptures of Moses (peace be upon him) are the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر).

وَالْمِيزانَ لِيَقُومَ النَّاسُ بِالْقِسْطِ and the Balance, so that people may maintain justice. [meaning] with justice (بالعدل). Al-Qummi said: The Balance (الْمِيزانَ) is the Imam (peace be upon him).

And in al-Jawami', it is narrated: That Gabriel (جبرئيل) descended with the Balance (الْمِيزانَ) and handed it to Noah (peace be upon him) and said: "Command your people to weigh with it."

** وَأَنْزَلْنَا الْحَدِيدَ فِيهِ بَأْسٌ شَدِيدٌ ** and We sent down iron, in which there is a great might. for the tools of war are made from it.

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): [It] means weapons (السلاح).

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him: His sending it down (انزاله) means His creation of it (خلقه له).

** وَمَنافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ ** and benefits for the people. since there is no craft (صنعة) except that iron is its tool (آلتها).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: That Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down four blessings (بركات) from the heaven to the earth: He sent down iron (الحديد), fire (النار), water (الماء), and salt (الملح).

** وَلِيَعْلَمَ اللهُ مَنْ يَنْصُرُهُ وَرُسُلَهُ بِالْغَيْبِ ** and so that Allah may ascertain who helps Him and His apostles in secret. [This is achieved] by using weapons (الاسلحة) in striving against the disbelievers (الكفّار). And [this phrase] is grammatically connected (العطف) to an omitted [verb] which is indicated by what precedes it, for it implies a reason (تعليلاً).

إِنَّ اللهَ قَوِيٌ ** Indeed Allah is All-strong, [meaning He is] capable of destroying whomever He wills to destroy. عَزِيزٌ ** All-mighty. [meaning He is] not in need of Himself (i.e., He is self-sufficient). And He only commanded them to strive (الجهاد) so that they may benefit from it and deserve the reward (ثواب) for compliance therein.

(٢٦) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا نُوحاً وَإِبْراهِيمَ وَجَعَلْنا فِي ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا النُّبُوَّةَ وَالْكِتابَ فَمِنْهُمْ ** (26) We certainly sent Noah and Abraham, and We appointed prophethood and the Book among their descendants. So among them [Some] of the progeny (الذرّيّة) مُهْتَدٍ وَكَثِيرٌ مِنْهُمْ فاسِقُونَ ** is guided, and many of them are transgressors. [They are] those who depart from the straight path (الطريق المستقيم) and turn away from the expected ways of conduct (سنن المقابلة), [this is stated] for the purpose of exaggeration in censure (الذم) and to indicate that dominance (الغلبة) belongs to error (الضلال).

(٢٧) ثُمَّ قَفَّيْنا عَلى آثارِهِمْ بِرُسُلِنا وَقَفَّيْنا بِعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ ** (27) Then We sent in their train Our apostles, and We sent in their train Jesus son of Mary, meaning, We sent a messenger after a messenger until it concluded with Jesus (peace be upon him). And the pronoun refers to Noah (peace be upon him) and Abraham (peace be upon him) and those to whom they were sent, or those messengers who were contemporary with them, not to the progeny (الذرّية), for the messengers who followed them were from the progeny (الذرّية). ** وَآتَيْناهُ الْإِنْجِيلَ وَجَعَلْنا فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ رَأْفَةً وَرَحْمَةً وَرَهْبانِيَّةً ابْتَدَعُوها ** and We gave him the Evangel, and We put kindness and mercy into the hearts of those who followed him. But as for monasticism, they innovated it— It is said that it refers to exaggeration (المبالغة) in worship (العبادة), spiritual discipline (الرياضة), and separation from people, attributed to the monk (الرّهبان), who is one who exaggerates in fear (الخوف) [of Allah] from وهب. In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Uyun, narrated from Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him), he said: The night prayer (صلاة اللّيل) ** ما كَتَبْناها عَلَيْهِمْ ** We did not prescribe it for them— We did not make it obligatory (فرضناها) upon them. ** إِلَّا ابْتِغاءَ رِضْوانِ اللهِ ** seeking only Allah’s pleasure. But they innovated it seeking Allah's pleasure. ** فَما رَعَوْها ** But they did not observe it meaning, they did not all observe it with the observance (رِعايَتِها) due to it, because of their denial of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. This is likewise mentioned in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله as a marfu' (elevated) narration. ** فَآتَيْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْهُمْ أَجْرَهُمْ وَكَثِيرٌ مِنْهُمْ فاسِقُونَ ** as was its due. So We gave the ones who had faith among them their reward, and many of them are transgressors. those who deviate (خارجون) from the following (الاتباع). In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Mas'ud, he said: I entered upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. He said: "O Ibn Mas'ud, those who were before you differed into seventy-two sects (فرقة). Two of them were saved, and the rest perished. One sect fought the kings concerning the religion of Jesus (peace be upon him), and the kings killed them. Another sect did not have the strength (طاقة) to confront the kings, nor could they remain among them calling them to the religion of Allah, the Exalted, and the religion of Jesus (peace be upon him). So they wandered (ساحوا) in the lands and became monks (ترهبوا). They are those concerning whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَرَهْبانِيَّةً ابْتَدَعُوها ما كَتَبْناها عَلَيْهِمْ (But as for monasticism, they innovated it—We did not prescribe it for them—)." Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Whoever believes in me, confirms me, and follows me, has observed it with the observance due to it (حقّ رعايتها). And whoever does not believe in me, those are the perished ones (الهالكون)." And in a narration, he said: The tyrants (الجبابرة) appeared over them after Jesus (peace be upon him), committing acts of disobedience to Allah. So the people of faith (أهل الايمان) became angry and fought them. The people of faith were defeated three times, and only a few of them remained. They said: "If we appear before these [tyrants], they will annihilate us (أفنونا), and none of those who believe will remain to call [others] to Him. So let us disperse throughout the earth until Allah sends the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله whom Jesus (peace be upon him) promised us," meaning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. So they dispersed into the caves (غيران) of the mountains and instituted monasticism (رهبانيّة). Among them were those who held fast to their religion, and among them were those who disbelieved. Then he recited this Ayah.

(٢٨) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَآمِنُوا بِرَسُولِهِ يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ ** (28) O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and believe in His Apostle. He will grant you a double share two portions (نصيبين) مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ وَيَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ نُوراً تَمْشُونَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** of His mercy, and appoint for you a light to walk by, and forgive you, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. Al-Qummi said regarding two portions (نصيبين) of His mercy: one of them is that He will not admit him to the Fire, and the second is that He will admit him to Paradise. and appoint for you a light means faith (الإِيمان). And in al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding a double share of His mercy, He said: Al-Hasan and Al-Husayn (peace be upon them both). And a light to walk by means an Imam (إماماً) whom you follow. And in al-Manaqib, He said: And the Light is Ali (peace be upon him).

(٢٩) لِئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ أَهْلُ الْكِتابِ ** (29) so that the People of the Book may know Meaning: So that they may know (ليعلموا), and the lā (لا) is superfluous (مزيدة).

** أَلَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلى شَيْءٍ مِنْ فَضْلِ اللهِ وَأَنَّ الْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ اللهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ. ** (29) that they have no power over any of Allah’s grace, and that all grace is in Allah’s hand, granting it to whomever He wishes, and Allah is the Possessor of the great grace.

In al-Majma' (المجمع) is what means: When His saying أُولئِكَ يُؤْتَوْنَ أَجْرَهُمْ مَرَّتَيْنِ بِما صَبَرُوا ** (Those will be given their reward twice for their patience) was revealed concerning the People of the Book who believed in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, those who had not believed in him heard this and boasted over the Muslims. They said: "O company of Muslims, as for those among us who believed in your Book and our Book, they have two rewards. And whoever among us believed in our Book [only], he has a reward like your rewards. So what superiority (فضلكم) do you have over us?" Then the verse يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (O you who have faith) was revealed.

And in a narration (رواية): Those among them who believed in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله—the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله—boasted and said: "We are superior to you; we have two rewards and you have one reward." Then the verse ** لِئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ ** was revealed.

In Thawab al-A'mal (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Whoever recites Surat al-Hadid (سورة الحديد) and Surat al-Mujadalah (المجادلة) in an obligatory prayer (فريضة) and persists in doing so, Allah will never punish him until he dies, and he will never see evil (سوء) befall himself or his family, nor any deficiency (خصاصة) in his body."

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: "Whoever recites all of al-Musabbihat (المسبّحات) before he sleeps will not die until he meets al-Qa'im (may the blessings of Allah be upon him). And if he dies [before meeting him], he will be in the proximity (جوار) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله."
